gene_description: Apolipoprotein B — primary structural protein of LDL particles (ApoB-100) and chylomicrons (ApoB-48). Essential for hepatic VLDL assembly and intestinal lipid transport. Variants affect LDL levels, abdominal obesity, and metabolic syndrome risk.

source.title: Gene-nutrient interactions and gender may modulate the association between ApoA1 and ApoB gene polymorphisms and metabolic syndrome risk
source.finding: APOB rs512535 major G allele homozygotes show increased metabolic syndrome risk through abdominal obesity and impaired insulin sensitivity, with significant gene-diet interactions — risk exacerbated by high-fat diets (>35% energy from fat), particularly in males.

GG.phenotype: Increased metabolic syndrome / abdominal obesity risk
GG.effect: Homozygous G carriers show increased metabolic syndrome risk driven by abdominal obesity and impaired insulin sensitivity. Effect is sex-specific (stronger in males). Risk exacerbated by high-fat diets (>35% energy from fat) and high monounsaturated fat intake (>14% energy). Does not directly affect dyslipidemia but influences adiposity patterns.
GG.recommendation: Limit total fat to <35% of energy intake. Reduce monounsaturated fat-heavy foods if excessive. Waist circumference monitoring. Focus on whole grains, fiber-rich foods. Males with this genotype should be especially vigilant about abdominal adiposity.

GA.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate risk profile. Mild gene-diet interaction with high-fat diets.
GA.recommendation: Balanced macronutrient distribution. Standard healthy diet with moderate fat intake.

AG.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate risk profile. Mild gene-diet interaction with high-fat diets.
AG.recommendation: Balanced macronutrient distribution. Standard healthy diet with moderate fat intake.

AA.effect: Homozygous minor allele carriers show lower metabolic syndrome risk from this locus. Less susceptible to fat-driven abdominal obesity.
